task_name: Add Custom Theme System to T3 Code Editor

plan_metrics.plan_content:
The plan is ready. Here's a summary of what I found and what I'm proposing:

---

## Plan Summary

**5 files total — no new dependencies, no build config changes.**

### What I discovered
- The existing `useTheme.ts` uses a clean `useSyncExternalStore` module-level store (no React Context) — I'll reuse this exact pattern
- CSS vars (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--accent`, etc.) are defined in `index.css` — inline `element.style.setProperty()` overrides them at highest specificity
- The Settings page already has a theme radio-group UI — `ThemeSelector` replaces it
- `packages/shared` uses direct `.ts` subpath exports (no build step needed)
- The `--alpha()` function in `index.css` is a Tailwind 4 compile-time function — theme colors must use standard CSS values (oklch/hex/rgba), which I've accounted for

### The 5 changes

| File | What |
|---|---|
| `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | New — `Theme`/`ThemeColors` interfaces, 3 built-in themes, `getTheme()`, `listThemes()` |
| `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `"./themes"` subpath export |
| `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | Rewrite — stores theme ID, injects CSS vars + `.dark` class, migrates old `"light"/"dark"/"system"` values, returns `{ theme, setTheme, availableThemes, resolvedTheme }` |
| `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | New — self-contained radio-group with color swatches per theme |
| `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Replace manual radio-group with `<ThemeSelector />` |
